items.title: India, South Africa eye NRR boost in close Group 1 race
items.summary: South Africa won the only T20I they played against Netherlands in 2010, while India hold a 20-3 head-to-head record against Bangladesh

items.title: Unsettled India look to keep campaign alive in Manchester
items.summary: Harmanpreet Kaur's side return to Old Trafford looking to kickstart another World Cup comeback, knowing another slip-up against Bangladesh could prove costly

items.title: Mushfiqur credits former trainer Karppinen for transforming his work ethic
items.summary: Heaping praise on Karppinen, the Bangladesh legend said that "nobody like him ever came", admitting that he still follows his advice

items.title: Sikandar Raza to miss Zimbabwe's Test against Bangladesh
items.summary: Richard Ngarava set for captaincy debut in the one-off Test between Zimbabwe and Bangladesh in Harare from June 28

items.title: Mushfiqur wins ICC Player of the Month award for May
items.summary: Mushfiqur was instrumental in Bangladesh’s 2-0 Test series victory, earning the Player of the Series award after scoring 235 runs at an average of 63.25. The 39-year-old struck a crucial 71 in the first innings of the opening Test in Dhaka before producing a match-defining 137 in the second Test in Sylhet.

items.title: Calculative risks pay off as Tigresses chase something more significant
items.summary: Buoyed by their win against Pakistan, Bangladesh women’s cricket team travelled to Manchester yesterday hoping to further their hopes of making it to the semifinals of the T20 World Cup.

items.title: Litton ruled out of Zimbabwe Test
items.summary: Bangladesh wicketkeeper-batter Litton Das has been ruled out of the one-off Test against Zimbabwe, scheduled to begin on June 28 in Harare, the Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) announced in a press release on Sunday.

items.title: Mahidul Islam replaces injured Litton Das for Zimbabwe Test
items.summary: Litton Das hasn't sufficiently recovered from the calf strain

items.title: Bangladesh bat first, make three changes in search of consolation win
items.summary: Australia named an unchanged side as they look to whitewash the T20 series

items.title: Pakistan 'defeated ourselves' against Bangladesh - Wahab
items.summary: Team mentor laments disastrous batting collapse after being 70 for 2 chasing 124

items.title: Shorna, spinners eliminate Pakistan from 2026 T20 World Cup
items.summary: After Shorna's cameo propelled Bangladesh, Nahida and Meghla took three wickets to help defend 123
